bugs.title: file_folders migration phase 2 pending
bugs.rootCause: Legacy routes still use file_folders instead of project_folders
verification: grep -R "file_folders" app lib

bugs.title: Project Hub Tier 1 analytics snapshot missing
bugs.rootCause: Tier-1 view lacked aggregate summary endpoint and snapshot card wiring
verification: Route /project-hub shows top-level snapshot card with aggregate metrics
GET /api/projects/summary returns totals, budget, work, and recentProjects

bugs.title: LocationMap uses deprecated DrawingManager
bugs.rootCause: LocationMap imported drawing library deprecated/removed in May 2026
verification: grep -n 'useMapsLibrary.*drawing' components/dashboard/LocationMap.tsx returns no results
grep -n 'DrawingManager' components/dashboard/LocationMap.tsx returns no results
Manual map drawing flows still function for all 7 tools

bugs.title: SlateDrop widget requires extra open click
bugs.rootCause: Widget shell CTA was rendered despite embedded SlateDrop body already visible
verification: Dashboard SlateDrop widget renders embedded file browser directly without redundant Open CTA
Project Hub SlateDrop widget renders embedded file browser directly

bugs.title: Location views inconsistent across contexts
bugs.rootCause: No shared location display contract/component
verification: Dashboard and Project Hub project cards use shared LocationDisplay component
Dashboard widgets API and Project Hub Tier-1 map cards use shared resolveProjectLocation helper
Wizard, dashboard location widget, and project cards render same location format

bugs.title: Trial tier entitlement misconfiguration — all modules granted
bugs.rootCause: lib/entitlements.ts TIER_MAP trial entry had all canAccess* flags set to true; trial should only access Hub + SlateDrop per module table
verification: grep -A 20 '"trial":' lib/entitlements.ts — confirm all canAccess* except canAccessHub and canViewSlateDropWidget are false
Trial user should see Upgrade prompt on any module tab

bugs.title: Type duplication: dashboard types defined in both DashboardClient and useDashboardRuntimeData
bugs.rootCause: DashboardWidgetsPayload, LiveWeatherState, DashboardProject, DashboardJob etc. defined identically in two files
verification: lib/types/dashboard.ts exists and both DashboardClient.tsx and useDashboardRuntimeData.ts import from it
No duplicate type definitions in either component file

bugs.title: Mock/demo data arrays in production DashboardClient component
bugs.rootCause: 8 demo data arrays (demoProjects, demoEvents, demoContacts, etc.) defined inline at lines 244-340 of DashboardClient.tsx violate Rule 9
verification: grep -n 'const demo' components/dashboard/DashboardClient.tsx returns no results
lib/dashboard/demo-data.ts exists and exports the arrays OR proper empty states are used

bugs.title: No error boundary on (dashboard) route group
bugs.rootCause: No app/(dashboard)/error.tsx exists; a runtime crash in any tab component unmounts the entire dashboard
verification: app/(dashboard)/error.tsx exists with reset function
Runtime error in a tab renders graceful fallback without dashboard unmount

bugs.title: Market Robot remains architecturally fragmented after March 11 UX/runtime pass
bugs.rootCause: Search, execution, and observability still span multiple partially overlapping systems: lexical Gamma search plus local filters, `market_plans`, legacy directives/runtime metadata fallbacks, `market_bot_runtime`, scheduler activity, trade persistence compatibility fallbacks, and wallet/CLOB prerequisites. Mar 12 Rescue Batch 1 fixed the shell/IA and truthful manual-buy fallback messaging, but it did not create a single canonical config/run/history model. Current runtime audit still shows one concrete live blocker: missing `NEXT_PUBLIC_POLYMARKET_SPENDER`.
verification: Run one authenticated end-to-end production test for each path: search, practice buy, live buy, apply plan, immediate scan, scheduled scan, results visibility
Document every storage/control surface touched by those paths and confirm whether one user action writes to more than one canonical source
Confirm whether Market Robot has a single run record linking user action, applied config, scan result, activity row, and trade write

Confirm whether requested product features still missing in code remain missing: recommendation-history store, moonshot history/bundles, posted-probability-vs-outcome tracking, hot-item/upset alert engine
Set NEXT_PUBLIC_POLYMARKET_SPENDER and verify live wallet approval path stops reporting backend blocker status

bugs.title: Platform Overlap: withAppAuth and hasBundleAccess bypass tier entitlements
bugs.rootCause: withAppAuth() and hasBundleAccess() only checked org_feature_flags raw columns, ignoring subscription tier — enterprise/business users with canAccessTourBuilder=true via tier were 403'd if standalone_tour_builder flag was false
verification: lib/server/api-auth.ts withAppAuth calls resolveOrgEntitlements, not loadOrgFeatureFlags directly
lib/server/org-feature-flags.ts hasBundleAccess calls resolveOrgEntitlements
lib/server/org-feature-flags.ts resolveOrgEntitlements fetches org tier + flags and delegates to getEntitlements()
app/tour-builder/page.tsx and app/site-walk/page.tsx use resolveOrgEntitlements

bugs.title: Visible Phase 1 owner path still showed legacy dashboard/projects/slatedrop surfaces after logic fixes
bugs.rootCause: The earlier Phase 1A logic and truth fixes did not fully reconcile the active owner-visible surfaces: logged-in shells still exposed `Dashboard` wording, `/projects` still presented legacy workspace composition, active Photos/Punch List surfaces still exposed customize affordances, and project-scoped SlateDrop still felt like a desktop widget shell on mobile.
verification: Active logged-in owner path labels say `Command Center` instead of `Dashboard` on dashboard/nav/account/install/slatedrop surfaces
`/projects` opens a simplified project-directory surface instead of the old portfolio/workspace composition
`/projects/[projectId]/photos` and `/projects/[projectId]/punch-list` no longer render visible customize controls

`/projects/[projectId]/slatedrop` renders a project-file shell with project-aware breadcrumb labeling, embedded list-default behavior, wrapped toolbar controls, and one-column mobile folder/file grids

bugs.title: Site Walk upload presign path bypassed SlateDrop reservation
bugs.rootCause: `/api/site-walk/upload` generated S3 keys in `site-walk/photos/...` without reserving a canonical `slatedrop_uploads` row in the project's `Photos` folder, so new captures could land outside the project file system until a later best-effort bridge ran.
verification: POST /api/site-walk/upload returns an S3 key under `orgs/{namespace}/{photosFolderId}/...` and a reserved `fileId`
POST /api/site-walk/items activates the reserved `slatedrop_uploads` row when the capture is saved
New Site Walk photos appear in the owning project's `Photos` folder via `slatedrop_uploads` instead of only under `site-walk/photos/...`

bugs.title: Site Walk plan image route missing
bugs.rootCause: `PlanViewer.tsx` rendered `/api/site-walk/plans/{id}/image`, but no server route existed to translate the stored `site_walk_plans.s3_key` into a browser-loadable image response.
verification: GET /api/site-walk/plans/{planId}/image returns a redirect to a signed S3 URL for a plan owned by the current org
Existing Site Walk plans render in `components/site-walk/PlanViewer.tsx` without changing the frontend src contract

bugs.title: Project helper APIs assumed org membership instead of project scope
bugs.rootCause: Recent files, records, photo reports, and management helper routes derived scope from `organization_members` lookups, so collaborator access could fail on secondary operations even when `project_members` access was valid.
verification: `app/api/projects/[projectId]/recent-files/route.ts` uses `withProjectAuth` and no longer fetches `organization_members` directly
`app/api/projects/[projectId]/records/route.ts` and `photo-report/route.ts` derive org scope from `withProjectAuth` instead of a separate org-membership query
`app/api/projects/[projectId]/management/contracts/route.ts` and `management/reports/route.ts` use the resolved project's `org_id` for storage/logging paths

bugs.title: R2 browser uploads blocked by missing bucket CORS policy
bugs.rootCause: After switching the shared storage client to Cloudflare R2, browser upload flows that PUT directly to presigned R2 URLs failed preflight because the bucket did not return `Access-Control-Allow-Origin` for the app origin. The app CSP was already updated; the missing piece was the Cloudflare bucket CORS rule.
verification: `npm run smoke:slatedrop-public-flow` completes the public upload portal flow without `No 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin' header` errors
Browser upload flows reach `/api/slatedrop/complete` after the presigned PUT instead of failing with `Failed to fetch`
Shared image/file flows still load correctly after the bucket CORS rule is added

bugs.title: SlateDrop share links drifted from live unified_files foreign key
bugs.rootCause: The repo still created and read share links through `slatedrop_uploads`, but the live database already enforced `slate_drop_links.file_id -> unified_files(id)`. Uploads succeeded, then share-link inserts failed because the uploaded file ID did not exist in `unified_files`.
verification: `app/api/slatedrop/complete/route.ts` creates or links a `unified_files` row for each activated SlateDrop upload
`app/api/slatedrop/secure-send/route.ts` inserts `slate_drop_links` rows with the bridged `unified_files.id`
`app/share/[token]/page.tsx` resolves shared files from `unified_files` first and still supports a legacy `slatedrop_uploads` fallback
`npm run smoke:slatedrop-public-flow` completes the public upload + share flow end-to-end

bugs.title: Public shell drifted away from the app brand token system
bugs.rootCause: The live homepage header/mobile sheet, shared public navbar/footer, app-detail pages, and branded portal still used hardcoded white, gray, zinc, and yellow classes plus repeated raw logo tags, so the public-facing product looked visually disconnected from the app despite the shared token system already existing.
verification: `components/marketing-homepage.tsx`, `components/Navbar.tsx`, `components/Footer.tsx`, `app/(public)/apps/[slug]/page.tsx`, and `app/(public)/portal/[token]/page.tsx` use semantic token classes instead of hardcoded white/gray/zinc/yellow shells
Shared public shells import `components/shared/SlateLogo.tsx` instead of repeating raw logo `` tags
`npm run typecheck` passes after the token migration
Production `main` deployment shows the same public shell styling as the branch build

bugs.title: PWA install icon used stale generic S favicon
bugs.rootCause: The manifest listed `/uploads/slate360-favicon-v2.svg` first and app metadata also referenced `/icon-v2.svg`; both SVGs still contained the old generic letter S mark while the PNG PWA icons had the updated geometric Slate360 mark.
verification: `app/manifest.ts` lists the correct PNG PWA icons before the SVG fallback
`app/layout.tsx` icon metadata includes `/uploads/icon-192.png` and `/uploads/icon-512.png` before `/icon-v2.svg`
`public/icon-v2.svg`, `app/icon.svg`, `public/uploads/slate360-favicon-v2.svg`, and `public/uploads/favicon.svg` contain the updated geometric Slate360 mark
After deployment, remove the old home-screen install and add Slate360 again to verify the OS uses the updated icon

bugs.title: Mobile refresh rendered unstyled page from stale service-worker caches
bugs.rootCause: Serwist default runtime caching and precaching could keep stale Next HTML/CSS/JS on mobile. After a deploy, a cached HTML document could reference retired CSS chunks, producing a mostly white text-only page on refresh.
verification: `app/sw.ts` is a kill-switch worker: it deletes all Cache Storage entries, posts `SLATE360_SW_KILL_RELOAD`, unregisters itself, and registers no fetch handler
`components/providers/SWRegistrar.tsx` registers `/sw.js` with `updateViaCache: 'none'` only until cleanup completes, then stores a disable flag and stops re-registering the worker
Generated `/sw.js` contains `2026-04-26-sw-kill-v2` and no old `static-style-assets` or `next-static-js-assets` runtime cache names
`npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` pass after the change
After deployment, mobile browser refresh should restore styled Slate360 pages and stay styled after 10+ seconds; if a tab is still controlled by the old worker, close/reopen once

bugs.title: Site Walk capture canvas reset during mobile markup
bugs.rootCause: The mobile capture canvas re-emitted markup on parent rerenders, reset local markup state when optimistic item updates changed `initialMarkup`, revoked/reused the active blob preview during upload reconciliation, and still allowed parent workspace swipe handling in the capture task mode. On phones this looked like a jumpy/scrambled canvas that reverted to the capture/data flow before pins or markup stuck.
verification: `PhotoMarkupCanvas` only emits markup when local `shapes` change and only resets local canvas state when `imageUrl` changes
`CameraViewfinder` preserves an existing blob preview when the server item replaces the local optimistic item
`CaptureClientIsland` passes `swipeEnabled={false}` to `PagedWorkspace` so Visual/Data navigation uses explicit Next/Back controls in capture mode
`npm run typecheck` passes after the stability fix
